breadcrumb: aN WELDING SPECIFICATION FOR FABRICATION OF (2 ALDIA > 1 SCOPE > Clause 1.1 This specification shall be followed for the fabrication of all types of welded joints of piping
text:
1.1. This specification shall be followed for the fabrication of all types of welded joints of piping
system with in battery limits of the plant .
The welded pipe joints shall include the following:
a. All pipe joints, longitudinal butt weld (for Tanks/ Plate works), circumferencial butt weld
and socket weld.
b. Attachment of forging, flanges and other supports to pipe.
c. Welded manifold header and other sub assemblies.
d. Welded branch connections with or without RF Pads
e. Joints in welded /fabricated piping components
f. The attachment of smaller connections for vents/drains/drips/and other instrument
tappings.
Any approval granted by Owner/CONSULTANT shall not relieve the Contractor of his
responsibilities and guarantee.

WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION
Welding procedure qualification shall be carried out in accordance with the relevant
requirements of ASME Sec. IX latest edition or other applicable codes and the job
requirements. The Contractor shall submit the proposed welding procedure specifications
required for the execution of the job in format as per Section IX immediately after the receipt
of the order. Owner/CONSULTANT inspector will review, check and approve the proposed
welding procedure submitted and shall release the procedure for qualification Record (PQR)
tests. The procedure qualification test shall be carried out by the Contractor at his own
expense. Acomplete set of test results in the format as per Section IX shall be submitted to
the Owner/CONSULTANT inspector for his approval immediately after completing the
procedure qualification test and at least 2 weeks before the commencement of actual work.
Standard test as specified in the code shall be carried out in all cases. In addition to these
tests, other test like macro/micro examination, hardness test, dye penetrant examination,

text: WELDER’S QUALIFICATION Welders shall be qualified in accordance with the ASME Section-IX or other applicable codes. The Owners inspector shall witness the test and certify the qualification of each welder separately. Only those welders who have been approved by the Owner / Consultant Inspector shall be employed for welding. Contractor shall submit the welder qualification test reports in the format as per Section IX and obtain express approval before commencement of work. It shall be the responsibility of Contractor to carry out qualification tests of welders. For welding of the steam piping, falling under the purview of Indian Boiler Regulations only those welders with IBR Certification, qualified by Boiler Inspectorate, and acceptable to the local Boiler Inspector authority shall be employed. The welders shall always have in their possession the identification card as issued by Contractor and shall produce it on demand by the Owner/ Consultant inspector. It shall be the responsibility of the Contractor to issue the identity cards after it has been duly certified by the Inspector. No welder shall be permitted to work without the possession of identity card. If a welder is found to perform a type of welding or in a position for which he is not qualified, he shall be debarred from doing any further work. All welds performed by an unqualified welder shall be cut and redone by a qualified welder at the expense of the Contractor. Welder performance record shall be made

breadcrumb: aN WELDING SPECIFICATION FOR FABRICATION OF (2 ALDIA > & WELDING SPECIFICATION FOR FABRICATION OF HALDIA > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING > Clause 10.2 Tack welds for maintaining the alignment of pipe joints shall be made only by qualified welders

using approved WPS. Defective tack welds must be removed prior to the actual welding of
the joints. Tacking cleats to be used in place of direct tacking (Pipe to Fittings / Pipe to Pipe )
and tacking clets shall be of same material as of pipe & fittings.

a. 3 Tacks — for 2” and smaller dia pipe
b. 4 tacks — for 3 to 12” dia pipes
c. 6 Tacks — for 14” and above larger pipes.

text: WPS. d. PWHT shall follow all welding, including support clips, and structural parts and repairs, but shall be performed prior to any hydro test or other load test. PWHT shall be carried out using one (1) or more of the following types of heat sources: Permanent or semi-permanent furnaces using gas or electric heaters. The furnace atmosphere shall be selected to avoid excessive oxidation during the heat treatment. Direct flame impingement by furnace burners or torch flames is prohibited. Electric resistance heaters. Thermocouples and a calibrated temperature chart auto recorder shall be used to provide accurate and legible record of all PWHT’s. All thermocouple attachments shall be adequately insulated to avoid temperature misreading caused by effect of radiation. Temperature recorders shall be calibrated every six (6) months and arecord shall be maintained on a recorder. The original calibration certificate shall be available for the review by Owner / Consultant. Prior to the start of PWHT, components shall be checked to ensure that all restraints are removed and the component is free to expand. Surface to the component subject to a heat treatment shall be dry and free from oil, grease, paint etc. All machined surfaces, such as flange faces, bolts holes, threads etc., shall be protected from oxidation during the heat treatment. All PWHT chart records shall be correctly identified and shall indicate as minimum: The weld number and the corresponding thermocouple number with their location. Heat treatment type. Temperature

After completion of the PWHT, all thermocouples shall be removed and the attachment areas shall be grounded smooth to clean sound metal and DPT to be carried out. Welding or heating after the final PWHT is not permitted without approval of Owner/ Consultant. The maximum variation in temperature within the heated zone shall not exceed 30°C during the soaking period. Weld hardness testing of production welds as required by ASME B31.3 shall be
performed in accordance with ASME B31.3.
i. Testing shall be conducted with portable hardness testers (Microdur or approved
equivalent).
ii. Contractor shall document the results of all hardness tests for review by Owner/
Consultant.
ili. All hardness readings in excess of values shown above shall be reported to Owner/
Consultant. Any corrective actions shall be reviewed with Owner/ Consultant,
before proceeding.

hardness testing, regardless of the service.
v. If PWHT is applied, hardness testing shall be performed after PWHT.

Contractor shall implementa material control system to ensure that materials can be identified
and traced from source to their installed location and associated with the appropriate
certification. 100% colour code implementation forthe material shall be ensured from vendor
shop for all piping materials. The methods of controlling the materials to be used and erected
at site are to be established and implemented at site. It includes requirements for receiving,
storing and releasing of all materials and equipment including the field purchased materials
for the construction.
Contractor supplied materials shall be with original, or verified copies of original certification.
Material Certificates shall be made available to Owner/Consultant for review.
Full traceability of piping materials shall be formally documented and maintained throughout
fabrication, painting, erection and test activities. Where piping materials have not been
maintained by traceability, Contractor shall undertake the necessary testing to demonstrate
that all the material used in the pressure loop is acceptable to specification, to the satisfaction
of the Owner/ Consultant. Testing may include PMI and/or the chemical analysis and
mechanical testing of samples. Consultant/ Owner has right to have any random material
sample tested at third party lab at contractor cost. 100% traceability is required for all lines
other than carbon steel within a specified temperature and pressure range.

text: PWHT. Additional NDE may be performed by Contractor prior to PWHT to ensure all reject able indications have been removed. Surface irregular, including cap welds, inhibiting accurate interpretation of the specified method of NDE shall be ground smooth. The minimum extent of examination shall be based on the requirements of ASME B31.3 and approved NDT Chart. Random examination on a percentage basis, for example 5% of pipe welds, is defined below: Examination is defined as 100% inspection of 5% of the production welds for each day production made by each welder. The first two (2) production welds made, by each welder, shall be included in the 5%. All welders, welding processes, pipe sizes and materials shall be represented as a minimum. The examination shall be concurrent with the fabrication. For each weld found to be defective, two (2) additional representative welds shall be examined at contractor’s own cost. In case these additional welds are free from defects, only the defects indicated in the first Examination shall be repaired and re- examined. However, if either of the two (2) additional welds shows defects, all welds represented shall be either: e Fully non-destructively examined and repaired as necessary, or ° Completely replaced. Any welder or welding operator having a weld rejection rate exceeding 10% of the deposited weld length shall be disqualified from the site or be subject to 100% non- destructive examination until the rejection rate reaches a level previously agreed

text: a. No welding joints shall be repaired more than twice. If the weld is still not acceptable after the second repair, Owner/ Consultant shall have the sole authority to permit additional repair attempt or to require the entire weld be cut out. Cracked welds shall be cut out and re-welded. Repair welding shall be performed using a properly qualified and approved procedure. In- process repairs (i.e. repairs performed prior to completion of the joint, using the same welding procedure as for the original fabrication) during production does not require a separate procedure. All repaired welds shall, as a minimum, be inspected using the original testing method. Additional test methods may also be required, if deemed necessary by Owner / Consultant. Damage to the base metal or welds, including dimensional changes caused by external forces, requires special repair and inspection procedures to be submitted to Owner / Consultant for review and approval prior to undertaking the repairs. Any weld not meeting the acceptance criteria of the referenced code or standard shall cut out or repaired. Repair of surface defects in welds by chipping, grinding and gouging are permitted provided the minimum required wall thickness plus corrosion allowance is maintained. Removal of defects shall be verified by magnetic particle or liquid penetrant examination. Repair welding of minor defects, as defined below, shall be done by qualified welders using a qualified welding procedure. Major defects in welds and base materials, as defined in

Repair welding of minor defects, as defined below, shall be done by qualified welders using a qualified welding procedure. Major defects in welds and base materials, as defined in below table shall be repaired only after a written procedure for each individual repair has been approved by Owner / Consultant. The written procedure shall include a sketch detailing extent and location of the defect, how defects will be removed, how removal will be verified, qualified welding procedure to be used, subsequent heat treatment and non-destructive examination.
